# UC-19.2.29 · Nutanix Controller VM Storage Bandwidth Saturation

## Description

When CVM front-end bandwidth saturates, latency rises for every VM on the node regardless of guest CPU. Spotting sustained saturation drives rebalance, network uplink upgrades, or noisy-neighbor containment.

## Value

When CVM front-end bandwidth saturates, latency rises for every VM on the node regardless of guest CPU. Spotting sustained saturation drives rebalance, network uplink upgrades, or noisy-neighbor containment.

## Implementation

(1) Collect per-CVM throughput and negotiated link speed; (2) alert when 95th percentile utilization exceeds 75% for one hour; (3) correlate with rebuild tasks (UC-19.2.9) and snapshot storms.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `TA-nutanix`, Prism metrics.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=hci` `sourcetype="nutanix:cvm:metrics"` with fields `node`, `read_mbps`, `write_mbps`, `link_speed_mbps`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Collect per-CVM throughput and negotiated link speed; (2) alert when 95th percentile utilization exceeds 75% for one hour; (3) correlate with rebuild tasks (UC-19.2.9) and snapshot storms.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=hci sourcetype="nutanix:cvm:metrics" earliest=-4h
| eval total_mbps=read_mbps+write_mbps
| eval util_pct=round(100*total_mbps/nullif(link_speed_mbps,0),1)
| stats perc95(util_pct) as p95_util by node
| where p95_util>75
| sort -p95_util
```

Understanding this SPL

**Nutanix Controller VM Storage Bandwidth Saturation** — When CVM front-end bandwidth saturates, latency rises for every VM on the node regardless of guest CPU. Spotting sustained saturation drives rebalance, network uplink upgrades, or noisy-neighbor containment.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=hci` `sourcetype="nutanix:cvm:metrics"` with fields `node`, `read_mbps`, `write_mbps`, `link_speed_mbps`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `TA-nutanix`, Prism metrics. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: hci; **sourcetype**: nutanix:cvm:metrics. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=hci, sourcetype="nutanix:cvm:metrics", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **total_mbps** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **util_pct** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by node** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where p95_util>75` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with Nutanix Prism (health, storage, and alerts) for the same cluster and time range.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (Mbps per node), Table (saturated CVMs), Gauge (peak utilization).

## SPL

```spl
index=hci sourcetype="nutanix:cvm:metrics" earliest=-4h
| eval total_mbps=read_mbps+write_mbps
| eval util_pct=round(100*total_mbps/nullif(link_speed_mbps,0),1)
| stats perc95(util_pct) as p95_util by node
| where p95_util>75
| sort -p95_util
```

## Visualization

Line chart (Mbps per node), Table (saturated CVMs), Gauge (peak utilization).
